contractSurface.securityClassification: platform-admin adaptive retrieval, deep research routing, evaluation, graph governance, and learning controls
contractSurface.identityNote: FLOW-29 keeps adaptive-rag-deep-research as the portability, UI, and package slug while the current runtime services live under server/src/engine/flows/rag-optimization. Fork assembly copies that implementation directory into the canonical package slug path.
coreGuarantees: Retrieval mode routing remains tenant scoped and persists routing decisions before emitting downstream events.
Vector, graph, and hybrid retrieval keep fabric-first access to database, RAG, queue, and AI providers.
Fusion, reranking, self-reflection, budget, context, and quality thresholds remain FREEDOM-driven rather than hardcoded.
Live query paths do not synchronously update learning policies.
Feedback ingestion and aggregation preserve six-tuple run context and trigger policy updates asynchronously.
Trace spans are emitted through queue fabric, with no direct observability SDK imports.
Domain profile builds, community summaries, and graph index rebuilds are asynchronous and versioned.
Graph edits and strategy promotion/rollback remain governance gated.
Improvement suggestions are human-gated and never auto-applied.
Control-plane node rendering reads all values from FREEDOM config or database fabric and handles empty config gracefully.

packageSigningConstraint.reason: FLOW-29 can steer retrieval, evaluation, and graph governance behavior for downstream generated work, so Tier 3 requires package-signing first pass.

services.className: Graph Governance Build Rollback And Control Plane Pipeline
machineLockedRules: graph edits are versioned and approval gated
multi-hop traversal keeps a visited-set guard
community summaries and index rebuilds run asynchronously
rollback swaps active pointers and does not delete versions
control-plane rendering handles empty FREEDOM config gracefully

configurationKeys.description: Tenant-editable FLOW-29 operating values. These values may tune retrieval posture, graph depth, fusion weighting, learning cadence, budget thresholds, evaluation posture, experiment behavior, control-plane display, and module naming without changing tenant isolation, fabric boundaries, governance gates, queue-only learning, or store-before-event ordering.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Safe tenant adaptations are limited to retrieval posture, depth and budget thresholds, evaluation strictness, learning cadence, control-plane labeling, and customer-facing module naming.

edgeCases.name: Acme research router
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant A tunes the module for platform-admin research routing with hybrid retrieval, conservative quality thresholds, and moderate graph depth.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: retrieval mode, graph hop limit, quality threshold, module naming
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Queue-only learning, tenant isolation, and governance gates remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Northwind knowledge ops
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant B tunes the installed package for operations knowledge search with tighter cost budgets and shorter feedback windows.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Budget hard-stop, fabric boundaries, and insert-only feedback storage remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Tessera research lab
edgeCases.semantic: Tenant C tunes the cascade package for deeper graph traversal and stricter self-reflection confidence.
edgeCases.extensionPoint: graph hop limit, context budget, self-reflection threshold
edgeCases.currentImplementation: Graph edits remain approval gated and rollback remains pointer-swap only.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenant adaptation cannot change.
rules: Task IDs T441 through T467 remain fixed.
Factory IDs F1176 through F1247 remain fixed.
Provider SDK access remains behind fabric interfaces.
Vault credential paths remain outside the package and outside tenant docs.
Routing, retrieval, feedback, graph, and evaluation reads remain tenant scoped.
Retrieval decisions and feedback records are stored before downstream events.
Learning policy updates are asynchronous and never part of the live request path.
Trace spans are emitted through queue fabric only.
Budget and quality gate violations remain business failures, not uncaught exceptions.
Improvement suggestions remain human-gated and never auto-applied.
Graph edits remain versioned and governance gated.
Multi-hop traversal keeps a visited-set guard.
Index rebuilds run asynchronously and live queries use the previous active index until swap.
Strategy rollback swaps active pointers and does not delete versions.
Control-plane rendering handles empty FREEDOM config as an empty state.

tenantAResearchRouter.description: Tenant A tunes Adaptive RAG / Deep Research for platform research routing.
tenantAResearchRouter.impact: Tenant A gets a stricter hybrid research router while tenant isolation, store-before-event ordering, queue-only learning, graph governance, and rollback semantics stay unchanged.
tenantAResearchRouter.testable: Fork CI and live auth checks still pass after tenant.config.json and freedom-config.defaults.json record these values.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: Elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: routing decision persistence before event emission
empty-state rendering for missing FREEDOM config

passCriteria: Tenant adaptations may change only declared FLOW-29 configuration values or display/module metadata.
FLOW-29 services remain MicroserviceBase subclasses after fork assembly.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing or cross-tenant token and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
Forked package CI must pass npm install, TypeScript, Jest, and provenance steps.
